# XFOIL insane: stored polar writeback

Wrap the `xfoil` executable, store two viscous polars in memory, and write them out later with `PWRT` when XFOIL is installed.

## Why This Case Exists

The top XFOIL rung should push farther into the interactive polar-management workflow instead of only writing files directly during accumulation.

## Case Shape

- ID: `xfoil_insane`
- Family: `xfoil`
- Difficulty: `insane`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `xfoil`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/xfoil_insane/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

This case relies on its workflow without extra breadth assertions.

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the real-world example lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=xfoil_insane
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only xfoil_insane
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

This case does not need checked-in fixtures.

## Workflow

### 1. Store two viscous polars in memory and write them later with PWRT

Tool: `run_xfoil`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "cwd": "{challenge_artifact_dir}",
  "stdin_text": "NACA 0012\nPANE\nPLOP\nG\n\nOPER\nVISC 200000\nALFA -2\nPACC\n\n\nASEQ -2 8 0.5\nPACC\nRE 150000\nINIT\nALFA -2\nPACC\n\n\nASEQ -2 8 0.5\nPACC\nPWRT\n1\npolar_saved_1.txt\nPWRT\n2\npolar_saved_2.txt\n\nQUIT\n"
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['polar_saved_1.txt', 'polar_saved_2.txt'].

## What This Case Proves

- A deterministic wrapper can cross from XFOIL polar accumulation into later stored-polar export commands.
- The challenge suite can retain multiple real outputs from a richer legacy CLI session than a single sweep.

## Known Limits

- This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `xfoil` is not installed.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: push XFOIL through stored-polar writeback.
# This top rung keeps the airfoil simple while exercising in-memory polar storage plus later PWRT export.
id = "xfoil_insane"
title = "XFOIL insane: stored polar writeback"
tier = "local_full"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"
family = "xfoil"
difficulty = "insane"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `xfoil` executable, store two viscous polars in memory, and write them out later with `PWRT` when XFOIL is installed."
motivation = "The top XFOIL rung should push farther into the interactive polar-management workflow instead of only writing files directly during accumulation."
proves = [
  "A deterministic wrapper can cross from XFOIL polar accumulation into later stored-polar export commands.",
  "The challenge suite can retain multiple real outputs from a richer legacy CLI session than a single sweep.",
]
limitations = [
  "This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `xfoil` is not installed.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["xfoil"]

[probe]
commands = [["xfoil"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_xfoil"
help_probe_args = ["-h"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Store two viscous polars in memory and write them later with PWRT"
tool = "run_xfoil"

[workflow.steps.arguments]
cwd = "{challenge_artifact_dir}"
stdin_text = """
NACA 0012
PANE
PLOP
G

OPER
VISC 200000
ALFA -2
PACC


ASEQ -2 8 0.5
PACC
RE 150000
INIT
ALFA -2
PACC


ASEQ -2 8 0.5
PACC
PWRT
1
polar_saved_1.txt
PWRT
2
polar_saved_2.txt

QUIT
"""

[workflow.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = ["polar_saved_1.txt", "polar_saved_2.txt"]
```
